title: Stress test: dimensional_chains.ri + query_consistency.ri
analysis:
This task creates two stress test suites exercising (1) the dimensional type system through complex unit chains, sqrt/root round-trips, and engineering formulas, and (2) geometric query consistency verifying mathematical relationships between query results.

Part 1 (dimensional_chains): The .ri language supports unit literals (mm, m, kg, s), dimensional arithmetic (mul/div add/sub exponents), and dimension-aware stdlib functions (sqrt halves exponents). The existing dimensional_consistency.ri + m8_stdlib_integration.rs tests prove this pattern works. This test will push deeper: 10-step chains (L→A→V→...→L), sqrt(area)→length round-trips, and complex engineering formulas (Reynolds number, moment of inertia, pressure, etc.).

Part 2 (query_consistency): The OCCT kernel provides Volume, SurfaceArea, Centroid, BoundingBox, Distance, and MomentOfInertia queries via GeometryKernel::query(). Spatial queries (contains, intersects) are NOT yet in the FFI layer. Tests will use real OCCT (guarded by OCCT_AVAILABLE) to verify mathematical relationships: sphere V≈4/3πr³, sphere A≈4πr², centroid of centered box at origin, centroid of translated box shifts correctly, and dimensional relationships between volume/area/length. For contains(solid, centroid(solid)) — since the contains FFI doesn't exist yet, mock-based tests will validate the expected eval flow.

Key patterns to follow: m8_stdlib_integration.rs for .ri-based eval tests (eval_ri_file helper), boundary4_geometry.rs for OCCT kernel tests (OCCT_AVAILABLE guard, OcctKernel::new()).

prerequisites.description: Create examples/dimensional_chains.ri fixture with 10-step unit chains, sqrt(area)->length round-trips, and complex engineering formulas (Reynolds number Re=ρvL/μ, moment of inertia I=mr², pressure P=F/A, power P=Fv, kinematic viscosity ν=μ/ρ, gravitational PE mgh, spring energy ½kx²)

steps.description: Write failing test: stress_dimensional_chains.rs — smoke test that dimensional_chains.ri parses, compiles, and evaluates without errors, producing non-empty values
Ensure dimensional_chains.ri fixture is correct and all expressions evaluate. Fix any parse/compile/eval errors in the .ri file until the smoke test passes

Write failing tests for 10-step unit chain assertions: verify each chain step produces the correct dimension (LENGTH→AREA via L*L, AREA→VOLUME via A*L, VOLUME→LENGTH via cbrt-equivalent, chain of force*distance=energy, energy/time=power, etc.) and that final values are numerically correct
Update dimensional_chains.ri if needed to ensure all 10-step chain computations produce expected dimensions and values. Tests should pass

Write failing tests for sqrt(area)→length round-trips: verify sqrt(w*h) has LENGTH dimension, sqrt(VOLUME*LENGTH) has AREA dimension, and sqrt(sqrt(L^4)) returns LENGTH. Also test that fractional dimension exponents work (sqrt of LENGTH gives LENGTH^(1/2))
Ensure .ri fixture has the sqrt round-trip expressions and they evaluate correctly. Fix any issues to make tests pass

Write failing tests for complex engineering formula dimensions: Reynolds number (dimensionless), moment of inertia (kg·m²), pressure (kg·m⁻¹·s⁻²), power (kg·m²·s⁻³), gravitational PE (kg·m²·s⁻²), and spring energy (kg·m²·s⁻²). Verify both dimension vectors and numerical values
Ensure .ri fixture engineering formulas produce correct dimensions and values. Fix any issues to make tests pass
Write failing test: stress_query_consistency.rs — sphere volume consistency: create sphere(r=0.05m) via OcctKernel, query volume, assert |V - 4/3·π·r³| < 1e-9. Guarded by OCCT_AVAILABLE
Implement sphere volume consistency test using OCCT kernel. Should pass immediately since it only queries existing kernel functionality
Write failing test: sphere surface area consistency — create sphere(r=0.05m), query SurfaceArea, assert |A - 4·π·r²| < tolerance. Verify A/V ratio ≈ 3/r
Implement sphere surface area test. Should pass with existing OCCT queries

Write failing test: centroid symmetry — create box(10mm,10mm,10mm) centered at origin, query centroid, assert centroid ≈ (0,0,0). Then translate box by (50mm,0,0), query centroid, assert centroid ≈ (0.055,0.005,0.005) shifted correctly
Implement centroid symmetry tests. Parse centroid JSON response and verify coordinates. Should pass with existing OCCT queries

Write failing test: Pappus-style volume/area relationships — for a box(w,h,d), verify V=w·h·d and A=2(wh+wd+hd) using Volume and SurfaceArea queries. Test with multiple box sizes to stress numerical precision
Implement Pappus-style box consistency tests. Should pass with existing OCCT queries

Write failing test: contains(solid, centroid(solid))=true using MockGeometryKernel — configure mock to return centroid inside bounding box, verify the eval pipeline handles query dispatch correctly. Also test distance(solid, solid)=0 for overlapping shapes
Implement mock-based contains/distance consistency tests. Configure mock geometry kernel with appropriate query results and verify correct dispatch

Fix review finding [precision_tolerance]: In box_volume_multiple_sizes (lines 305-312, 323-330), replace absolute tolerance 1e-15 with relative tolerance assertion: assert!((v - expected).abs() / expected < 1e-10). The 1e-15 absolute tolerance demands relative error <8e-9 for a 5mm cube (V=1.25e-7 m³), which is below the precision floor of double-precision floating-point operations accumulated through OCCT's BRep geometry pipeline and mm→m unit conversions. Relative tolerance 1e-10 is robust across all volume magnitudes and consistent with the project's tolerance conventions (1e-9 to 1e-12 used elsewhere in the file).
Verify the updated box_volume_multiple_sizes test compiles and passes with the new relative tolerance assertions. Run the full stress_query_consistency test suite to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Split dimensional_chains into .ri fixture + Rust harness, query_consistency as pure Rust kernel tests
design_decisions.rationale: Dimensional chains exercise the .ri language's type system and are best tested as .ri source through the full parse→compile→eval pipeline (matching m8_stdlib_integration.rs pattern). Query consistency tests exercise geometry kernel API directly since measurement functions aren't yet .ri-callable stdlib entries — they operate through GeometryKernel::query() at the Rust level.

design_decisions.decision: Use real OCCT kernel for geometry query tests (guarded by OCCT_AVAILABLE)
design_decisions.rationale: Mock-based geometry tests don't verify mathematical relationships since we control the mock outputs. Real OCCT gives us genuine mathematical consistency checks (V=4/3πr³ for spheres, centroid at center of symmetric shapes, etc.). The OCCT_AVAILABLE guard ensures tests skip gracefully on systems without OCCT.

design_decisions.decision: Use mock kernel for contains(solid, centroid(solid)) test
design_decisions.rationale: The FFI layer doesn't have point_in_solid/contains yet (task 319's spatial queries aren't in the OCCT FFI bindings). We use MockGeometryKernel with configured query results to validate the eval pipeline's query dispatch flow, providing a scaffold for when the real contains FFI is wired.

design_decisions.decision: Engineering formulas expressed using unit arithmetic rather than user-defined type aliases
design_decisions.rationale: The .ri language constructs derived units via arithmetic: `let n_unit = 1kg * 1m / (1s * 1s)` for Newton. This matches the existing pattern in math_linalg.ri and dimensional_consistency.ri, and exercises the dimension multiplication/division logic directly rather than relying on type alias infrastructure.

design_decisions.decision: 10-step chain uses a single structure with progressive let-bindings
design_decisions.rationale: Each chain step builds on the previous via let-bindings, creating a dependency chain that exercises both the dimensional type system and the eval engine's topological ordering. This is a more realistic stress test than isolated expressions.

design_decisions.decision: Use relative tolerance for OCCT volume assertions on small geometries
design_decisions.rationale: Absolute tolerance 1e-15 for volumes ~1e-7 m³ demands relative error <8e-9, below the precision floor of double-precision floats after BRep pipeline and mm→m conversions. Relative tolerance (v - expected).abs() / expected < 1e-10 scales correctly across all volume magnitudes and avoids false failures from accumulated ULP rounding errors.

reuse.how: Copy the helper that loads a .ri file, parses, compiles (asserting no errors), evaluates with MockConstraintChecker, and returns EvalResult. Same pattern: fs::read_to_string → parse → compile → Engine::new(checker, None) → eval.
Each test starts with `if !reify_kernel_occt::OCCT_AVAILABLE { eprintln!("skipping..."); return; }` and then creates OcctKernel::new() directly.
Use MockGeometryKernel::new().with_query_result(handle_id, expected_value) to configure mock responses for query dispatch testing.

reuse.what: Value constructors from reify-test-support/values.rs (mm, meters, mm2, mm3)
reuse.how: Use mm(), meters(), mm2(), mm3() for constructing test values with correct SI conversions and dimension vectors.

reuse.what: DimensionVector constants and operations from reify-types
reuse.how: Use LENGTH, AREA, VOLUME, MASS, TIME, FORCE constants and .mul()/.div()/.root()/.pow() methods to construct expected dimension vectors for assertion comparisons.
Follow same .ri structure: single `structure def` with let-bindings for computed values, unit helpers like `let n_unit = 1kg * 1m / (1s * 1s)`, and inline comments explaining expected results.

reuse.what: Centroid JSON parsing from boundary4_geometry.rs translate_centroid test
reuse.how: Centroid returns Value::String with JSON {"x":f64,"y":f64,"z":f64}. Parse with string matching or minimal JSON parsing to extract coordinates.
